title: Vettangudi Bird Sanctuary
tagline: Vettangudi Bird Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Vettangudi Bird Sanctuary is a 0.384 km2 (0.148 sq mi) protected area, declared in June 1977 near Thirupattur in the then Ramnad District and present day Sivaganga District that in
overview.description: Vettangudi Bird Sanctuary is a 0.384 km2 (0.148 sq mi) protected area, declared in June 1977 near Thirupattur in the then Ramnad District and present day Sivaganga District that includes the periya kollukudi patti, chinna kollukudi patti, and vettangudi patti irrigation tanks. The heaviest rainfall occurs between October and December, when the northeast monsoon brings in 330–390 mm (13–15 in) of rainfall.

topPlaces.name: Chokkanathapuram, Tamil Nadu
topPlaces.description: Chokkanathapuram is a small village in Sivaganga district of Tamil Nadu state in India.

topPlaces.name: Muraiyur
topPlaces.description: Muraiyur is a village in the Taluk of Singampunari in the district of Sivaganga in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. Distance from other locations: 9.566 kilometres (5.944 mi) from the Taluk's main town of Singampunari 33.15 kilometres (20.60 mi) from the district's main city of Sivaganga 375 kilometres (233 mi) from the state capital of Chennai.

topPlaces.name: Semmanipatti
topPlaces.description: Semmanipatti, also called Semminipatti or Chemminipatti, is a village in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. Situated at a distance of 29 kilometres (18 mi) North of Madurai and 3 kilometres (1.9 mi) south of Vadipatti on the National Highway (NH 7).

topPlaces.name: Mampatti
topPlaces.description: Mampatti is a village in Singampunari Taluk in Sivagangai District of Tamil Nadu State, India. It comes under Mampatti South panchayath union.

topPlaces.name: Sadurvedamangalam
topPlaces.description: Sadur Veda Mangalam is a village in Singampunari Taluk, Sivaganga district, Tamil Nadu, India, 400 kilometers from the state capital of Chennai.

topPlaces.name: Sowmya Narayana Perumal temple
topPlaces.description: Sowmyanarayana Perumal Temple is an ancient temple located in Thirukoshtiyur, a village in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu, dedicated to the Hindu god Vishnu. Constructed in the Dravidian style of architecture, the temple is glorified in the Nalayira Divya Prabandham, the early medieval Tamil canon of the Alvar saints from the 6th–9th centuries CE. It is one of the 108 Divya Desams dedicated to Vishnu, who is worshipped as Sowmyanarayana Perumal and his consort Lakshmi as Thirumamagal.

topPlaces.name: Eriyur
topPlaces.description: Eriyur is a village in Singampunari Taluk in Sivaganga district of Tamil Nadu, India. It is a large village consisting of two Panchayat villages: Eriyur and Mampatti Devasthanam.

topPlaces.name: Jeyankondanilai
topPlaces.description: Jeyankondanilai is a head village in singampunari taluk, Sivaganga district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. This village is predominantly Kallar village with a minor section of SC community and a couple of families of other castes.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Vettangudi Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Vettangudi Bird Sanctuary is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Vettangudi Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Madurai Airport (55 km) and the nearest railway station is Madurai Junction (48 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Vettangudi Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Vettangudi Bird Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Vettangudi Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Vettangudi Bird Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Vettangudi Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Chokkanathapuram, Tamil Nadu, Muraiyur, Semmanipatti, Mampatti, Sadurvedamangalam.
